entry.title: Internal Hiring: Recruiting-to-Employee Central
entry.content:
Requirement

Implement Crossboarding alongside Onboarding 2.0 in SAP SuccessFactors to facilitate internal transitions and employee movements.

Limitations of Crossboarding

While implementing the crossboarding solution for one of the businesses, we encountered several issues that could not be resolved using the crossboarding module. Some of the key challenges include:

  • The HR team cannot review crossboarding letters before they are sent to the candidate or internal employee.
  • When a candidate is moved to the Onboarding bucket and onboarding is initiated, data is automatically published to Employee Central. This results in incomplete or inaccurate records in EC(even before crossboarding is completed), disrupting the smooth transition of employees.
  • Compensation and Job Information data are being overwritten if not transmitted from the Recruiting system, leading to confusion and potential data loss.
  • Crossboarding 2.0 allows only a limited set of pay components to flow from the Recruitment Management module to Employee Central. This creates a challenge in capturing the full compensation details accurately, especially when additional pay components are needed.
Solution – Internal Hiring via Recruitment Management

The challenge with crossboarding arises as soon as onboarding is triggered. To resolve this, we can bypass the onboarding trigger and exclude internal hires from the crossboarding process. Instead of utilizing crossboarding, internal employees can be directly hired from Recruiting into Employee Central. This can be achieved by introducing a custom filter field that distinguishes between job requisitions for internal and external hires. This field will determine whether the hiring process follows the Recruiting-to-Employee Central route or the Recruiting-to-Onboarding-to-Employee Central route.

Implementation Steps

In order to implement the internal hiring process using Recruiting-to-Employee Central, below are the steps to be implemented. The diagram below shows a quick overview of the process.

 

1. Add a Filter Field on the Job Requisition Details Page.

Navigate to Manage Templates -> Recruiting Management -> Job Requisition -> Select the relevant Job Requisition Template -> Add the Standard Filter Field (e.g., filter1). For example, the field could be labeled "Type of Hire" (Internal Hiring / External Hiring).

The "Type of Hire" field will be a picklist.

Ensure the appropriate view and edit permissions are granted to stakeholders based on business requirements. In this example, HR and Recruiters are given edit access, while all other stakeholders have view-only permissions.

You can configure additional filters in the Onboarding Integration Setup page by defining filter fields in the job requisition template. There are up to 15 custom filter fields (filter1-15) that can be created in the job requisition template.

Sample Field Definition of Filter Fields:

<field-definition id="filter1" type="picklist" required="false" custom="true">
<field-label><![CDATA[Type of Hire]]></field-label>
<picklist-id>test_ONBreq_RCM</picklist-id>
</field-definition>

 

 

 

 

2. Add the filter field under custom filters Internal and External Career Search Settings.

Go to Internal and External Career Search Settings and locate the custom filters section. For the filter field created (filter1), update the field label and picklist ID accordingly. Ensure this update is applied under both the External and Internal Site Tabs.

 

3. Update the field Criteria for Onboarding Integration

In the Settings tab, you can enable Onboarding either for all job requisitions or for specific ones. Use the available dropdown fields to define the criteria for job requisitions where Onboarding will be applied.

Navigate to Onboarding Integration Setup and select the radio button labeled "Apply Onboarding to Job Requisitions that meet ANY of the following criteria."

The first dropdown field will display the available filters for the requisition, and the second dropdown will show the corresponding values for those filters. Select "filter1" and choose the appropriate value to trigger the onboarding process.

 

 

 

 

This means that Onboarding will only be triggered if the "Type of Hire" is set to "External Recruit." If the "Type of Hire" is set to "Internal Recruit," the Initiate Onboarding button will not be available. Once the candidate is moved to the Onboarding status, they will directly appear under Manage Pending Hire – Recruitment.

Please ensure that the filter value is correctly updated in the job requisitions for the system to validate and apply the filter and integration settings.

 

 

 

4. Create Internal Hiring templates.

Offer letter and offer approval templates in Recruiting can be tailored specifically for the internal hiring process. These templates function similarly to those for external hires, following the same process for sending letters and obtaining offer approvals.

Incorporating these additional steps into your external hiring process will enable the internal hiring process to follow the same structure, ensuring consistency while preventing data loss and issues for internal employees.

Solution Benefits

  • The direct Recruiting Management to Employee Central solution ensures that existing employee data, such as pay components and other compensation details in EC, are preserved, preventing the loss of critical information during the internal transfer process. During Manage Pending Hire, HR has the opportunity to validate everything before updates are made to EC.
  • Only the data updated during the recruitment process is reflected in Manage Pending Hire; all other information remains unchanged in EC.
  • HR teams can review offer letters and new contracts before they are sent to the candidate, ensuring accuracy and compliance.
  • Data is transferred to EC only after the internal hiring process is finalized in the Manage Pending Hires tool. This prevents premature publication of information in EC, ensuring that all data is complete and accurate before the employee's new role is updated in the system.

Please note, that if workflows in Employee Central are triggered by data changes, they will be activated when changes are submitted in Manage Pending Hire. These workflows should be adjusted to avoid triggering during the internal hire process if not required by the business. Additionally, the Event and Event Reason fields must be manually filled in Manage Pending Hire.

Conclusion

Implementing a direct Recruiting Management to Employee Central process for internal hires offers a reliable and efficient solution, bypassing the complexities of Crossboarding. By tailoring the hiring process with custom filters, internal hiring templates, and thorough validation in Manage Pending Hires, businesses can ensure accurate data transfer and minimize errors. This approach preserves critical employee information, enhances HR oversight, and simplifies the overall internal mobility process. With the right configurations, companies can reduce administrative overhead, improve data integrity, and provide a positive experience for both HR and employees during internal transitions.

By adopting this solution, organizations can optimize their internal hiring workflows, fostering smoother transitions and better management of employee data within SAP SuccessFactors.

This solution was accepted and was implemented for the business. Hope this helps you to define an alternative in case you are facing similar issues.

Please feel free to drop a comment for any questions or queries or share your way of handling these problems. Thank you for reading this blog. Happy Learning 🙂

entry.title: Transforming Your HR Operating Model with SAP SuccessFactors Implementation
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business environment, staying ahead requires not just innovation but a keen understanding of operational processes, technology, and adaptable operating models.

On Thursday 12th September 2024, Dr Andreas Eckert, Global HR Advisor, and Klaus Kampen, SAP’s Business Architect for HR Innovation and Transformation, delved into the potential impact and opportunities for your HR operating model through the implementation of SAP SuccessFactors. 

For us at SAP, success is when customers actually realize the value that they expect from their investment in our solutions. But obviously value doesn't realize itself. A solution itself doesn't provide you with the value. It's important to design the processes that actually drive the system configuration, with some principles in mind on how they best perform the best in your specific context. 

Important processes though, are not only just things that happen along a chain of a chain of events. Processes also include the actors that are actively contributing and doing tasks along these processes. So the operating model of HR informs and must inform the process design. And this is where the concept of SAP’s leading practices comes in.

SAP SuccessFactors has a number of leading practices across all solution areas that actually encapsulate and condensate consulting experience and combine that with product development and design principles to inform process design to inform the operating model duties and help move forward. 

The webinar focussed on the 1st building block out of the critical steps chain that need to be taken to arrive to the point of value: 

Key Insights from the Webinar:

  • Learning from Top Performers: Using a sports example, we explored how leading organizations use SAP SuccessFactors to streamline HR processes and drive business success.
  • Impact of Leading Practices: SAP SuccessFactors’ best practices provide a strong foundation for transforming HR functions across consultancy, expertise, and operations. (Independently from your particular HR Operating Model) There are 5 essential functions that exist in all HR Operating Modell that assume responsibilities and that will be impacted by the implementation of SAP SuccessFactors
  • Real-Life Customer Example: We demonstrated how a customer successfully applied SAP SuccessFactors leading practice recommendations to overcome manual processes, scale HR operations, and align with their growth strategy.
  • Workshop Insights: Through our Impact and Opportunity workshops, we highlighted the importance of mapping roles and responsibilities to ensure clarity and efficiency in process design. 
  • Process Design and Configuration: We emphasized how defined tasks and roles translate into detailed process diagrams, which guide the technical configuration of SAP SuccessFactors.
  • HR Leading Practice Advisory Service: We introduced our advisory service, which supports organizations in aligning their HR models with SAP SuccessFactors best practices for a smooth and tailored implementation.

The implementation of SAP SuccessFactors can elevate your HR operations by aligning HR with business goals, automating key processes, enhancing employee experience, and enabling data-driven decision-making. Organizations that are prepared to embrace this change will unlock new opportunities for growth, innovation, and efficiency.

HR Leading Practices Advisory Service is part of the SAP SuccessFactors Services portfolio that aims to support customers interested in applying leading practices to elevate their own Operating Model and achieve value expected from the SAP SuccessFactors features and function by: 

  • Defining the right HR concepts and select the right scope 
  • Evaluating the potential change impact on your HR organization
  • Prepare a smooth SAP SuccessFactors implementation with a well- equipped project team that has made key design decisions in advance 

If you are interested in learning more, please don’t hesitate to reach out to: SAPHIT@SAP.com

Resources: 

Webinar Recording

Slides

entry.title: Original Start Date, Hire Date, Seniority Date. What’s the difference?
entry.content:
Original Start Date, Hire Date, Seniority Date. What’s the difference?

 

“Original Start Date”

The “Original Start Date” field is used to store the very 1st Hire date for an Employee when hiring an employee via the “Add New Employee”, the “Manage Pending Hires” or the “Manage Inactive Employee” wizards.

 

It gets populated with the 1st Hire record Effective Start Date that an employee account receives when he/she is Hired into the system.

“Hire date”

This initial hire date comes from the Hire Date field but…

 

If the employee is Terminated and Rehired, their hire date field under “Employment Details” section will be updated to display the employee's latest hire record.  

“Seniority start date”

This date field can change as per the company's policy.

 

E.g.: There may be a policy that the seniority date will start once the Employee completes probation of 6 months. In this case the hire date will remain as the employee's hire date, however “Seniority Date” can be updated to the hire date + 6 months.

 

Original Hire date: What happens if I “rehire with old employment.”?

The Original Start Date field usually gets populated by the Effective Start Date of the Job Info "Hire" record (with the Hire event) - the very first time the employee is hired.  

The system will automatically correct the “Original Start Date” to when the first Job Info Hire record is re-imported.

 

Original Hire date: What happens if I “rehire with new employment.”?

When you rehire an inactive employee with the new employment wizard, the Original Start Date will not be same as old employment's Original Start Date, because the new employment will have new user id and Original start date for new employment will have the Date as First Jobinfo record with Hire event.

 

Seniority Start Date, what’s SuccessFactors standard behavior?

For new hires, the seniority start date field will have the same date as the hire date.

For rehires, the seniority start date will default from the rehire date due to the following:

When rehiring the user, the system disregards the seniority that was built up in the previous employment and starts from scratch as of the rehire date.

 

Condensed links

2080126 - How do Employment Information > Hire Date and Original Start Date fields work? - Employee Central

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2080126

2948707 - EC: Difference between SeniorityDate and HireDate in Employment Details

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2948707

2487332 - Why is Seniority Start Date Defaulted From Rehire Date

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2487332

Changing an Employee's Hire Date in Employee Central

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_EMPLOYEE_CENTRAL/4b0f4a72b0ea46fb9b9917bdd7c8f29b/722047f75ba54c9198b273c031a93422.html

entry.title: Creating a Pay Component with monthly amount based on other Pay component with annual amount
entry.content:
Hi there, came with another blog, wherein we got a requirement to populate Pay component 1 (say Annual Salary) with annual amount to a another Pay component 2 (say Annual Salary – Monthly for ECP) with monthly amount.

This requirement came into effect as “Payroll team” want the amount in “Monthly figure” whereas client want the amount to be displayed as “Annual figure” on employee’s profile.

We have met this requirement with business rule and placed this rule as “OnSave” on compensation information section under Manage Business Configuration.

First step is to create both pay components via Manage org job and pay structures.

  • Go to Manage org job and pay structures. Click on create new. Create Annual Salary pay component. Below is the screenshot of the same.

 

 

 

  • Similarly, create another pay component 2 (Annual Salary – Monthly for ECP) with “MON” frequency
  • After creating both pay components, provide respective RBPs.

 

Then we will start creating the business rule. Base object of the rule is “Compensation Information Model”.

The rule says that when Amount is not equal to Null where pay component is Annual salary, then create a new record with pay component as (Annual Salary – Monthly for ECP) where frequency is “MON”.

Amount is calculated with Divide function, where the dividend is “Amount of the Annual Salary” and Divisor is “12”.

 

 

This rule is placed as “On Save”.

So, now when you add “Annual Salary” on employee profile under Compensation section, then automatically “Annual Salary – Monthly for ECP” will be created.

Thank you for reading the blog. I hope this will help you in meeting certain requirements. Your feedback, questions, and suggestions are always welcome.  We’d love to hear about your experiences, custom requirements, or any insights you have, so please feel free to share them in the comments section below. Happy Learning

entry.title: How to Prorate Accruals When Employees Switching Between Regular and Shift Roles?
entry.content:
Managing employee accruals can be complex, especially when employees transition between regular and shift roles within the same year. Understanding how to accurately prorate these accruals is crucial to maintaining accuracy and consistency. Here's a one such example.

Requirement:

When an employee is in a regular role, accruals are determined based on a seniority lookup table. For shift workers, however, the accrual balance is reset to zero.

When an employee transitions between regular and shift roles within a year, the accruals need to be prorated and recalculated accordingly.

For rounding purposes, amounts should be rounded to the nearest 0.5. For example, any amount between 0.01 and 0.50 should be rounded up to 0.5, while amounts between 0.51 and 1.0 should be rounded up to 1.0.

Solution:

  1. We need a custom field on job info to store annual leave entitlement based on whether employee is regular or shift employee. This field will be automatically updated whenever job information is saved.

  1. Accrual Rule– Function to be used in rule-‘Calculate Average Value For Numeric Job Info Field()’ –This rule function calculates the average value of any numeric job information field based on months (that takes care of number of transfers employee might have had during a year). You can prorate or recalculate the leave accrual value depending on the changes to the job information field.

entry.title: Block leave counter solution in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central
entry.content:
Welcome viewers, it's glad to see you again.

This is one of the most complex scenarios and I tackled with my own solution. 

In this blog I am discussing the configurations of block leave counter which is developed in the demo system.

What is Block leave?
          When an employee applies an annual leave for 10 days or more than that consequently, it is considered as Block leave. Days differs according to your customer requirement.

Yes, I know you were searching for this blog as this is one of the important requirements where customer may request as an essential requirement. I will dive into the configuration part.

Step 1: Creating a custom fields in the Employee Time object definition

Create custom fields as per your requirement in the configure object definition Employee Time
object. Below image is for your reference.

This field was designed to allow employees and HR to indicate whether an employee’s leave request should be blocked under specific conditions. If this field need to display only selecting annual leave. You can configure as below using field value condition and time type code in your custom field.

 

Step 2: Creating a Rule (Block Leave Counter) in Time Type

I have created 3 rules for my scenario and assigned in the time type, start date and end date field. And additionally, 2 rules assigned in the manage data --> time type --> take rules.

  1. Create Block Leave Counter Rule:
    • Create a new rule called Block Leave Counter. This rule will check multiple conditions to block the leave request.
  2. Navigate to Manage Data:
    • Go to Admin Center and access Manage Data.
  3. Select Time Type:
    • Search for the relevant Time Type that is used for leave requests.
  4. Go to Take Rule Section:

Within the selected Time Type, locate the Take Rule section where rules for leave management are configured.

Rule 1. Take Rules under ---> Manage Data (Time Type)

Rule 2. Take Rules under ---> Manage Data (Time type)

Below rules were assigned in the time type, start date, end date fields in the configure object definition.
Rule 3.

Rule 4.

Rule 5 Set Rule on Save:

  • In the EmployeeTime object, configure the rule to trigger on Save. This rule will be executed when a record in the EmployeeTime object is saved. Even if an employee cancelling the leave, block leave will set as 0. where this will avoid the use of integration center to update the leave balance.

Test Output Cases are below.

Before submitting screenshot is below. On selecting 10 days, block leave field will be shown for employee alone as Yes. Another field Block leave status will be marked as yes only after the approval where HR confirm this. 


Once leave approved, output will be as below.

On cancelling leave, block leave will be reset as per rule.



On next time, if employee try to apply leave in same year, block leave status will be yes, as he already took the block leave in this current year. Else the block leave status will be no as default on initiating the leave.

Thanks for referring to my blog. Will reach you in next blog with a more interesting scenario.

Regards,
Aravind R
HXM consultant.

entry.title: New 2H 2024 SAP Successfactors Time (Tracking) Features
entry.content:
My dear Time Management Community!

It is kind of tradition since years that you get from me as part of the Successfactors Time Management Product management Team an overview on new delivered features. This comes for better comprehension with some business background and configuration insights. And here we go - as in the old tradition you get now some words on the new Time Tracking / Management features for the 2H 2024 release. There are again lots of cool and good features - I want to just call out the Time Statement as a teaser. Many customers are eagerly awaiting this functionality. But there is of course much more in the shipment box. 

However, before going into details: any tradition has got a big sister called history. And the transition from one to the other is often gradually, but sometimes fast. My tradition turns now rather fast into history. This blog will be the last one you get from me. It was a big joy and pleasure writing up this blogs and helping you understand the features and functions that our engineering teams develop. Thanks a lot for accompanying me and the product evolution in the last 10 years.

And now back to tradition ;-). As always, you can just get an overview or deep dive in the respective chapters if you are interested in a specific feature. Or, for the very interested and brave ones, read the whole blog.

 

Overview:


1. Time Tracking Features

1.0 Time Statement

Possibility for employees to have all time data, valuation results, time account balances and alerts for a period with one click available for a month (or week)

1.1 Visualization of alerts and errors in time sheet on the respective day

Big usability improvement in time sheet UI: alerts and errors are now not only displayed as a summary in a pop-up, but on the respective day on which they occured

1.2 Employee Self Service for temporary change of work schedule

Ever wanted empower employees to change temporarily their work schedule ? This can now be done for a day or a period and of course with approval workflow

1.3 Employee Self Service for temporary change of public holiday calendar

Is your employee going onto a business trip where a different public holiday calendar is valid? He can now request this change via an ESS scenario.

1.4 General availability of monthly time sheet UI and monthly submit period

Now available for all Time Tracking customers: the monthly calendar time sheet UI plus monthly approval period

1.5 Display of previous values in time sheet amendments

Supports a manager in the approval process for time sheet amendments due to the fact that previous values of the original time sheet are shown. With this he can easily detect what an employee has changed in the time sheet

1.6 Time Sheet submit attestation possibility

You can now configure a dedicated text box that employees need to accept upon time sheet submit

1.7 Time container display of different periods in time sheet UI

Up to now only weekly or monthly results have been shown in the time sheet UI. But now you can also see for example bi-weekly or yearly overtime results in a weekly or monthly time sheet

1.8 Time container integration to Payroll

Our new time containers can now be replicated to ECP Payroll or SAP onprem Payroll. 

1.9 Time Container availability in integration center

Time containers are now available in the integration center

1.10 Time Sheet Approval Center enhancements

You can now configure time containers and allowances as key facts for the time sheet approval center and some more enhancements

1.11 Split and delimit of temporary time information

Overlapping temporary change of workschedule information did lead up to now to an error and a manual deletion was necessary. Now we provide and automated split and delimit for it.

1.12 General availability of Business rules in time valuation

From restricted availability to general available for any Time Tracking customer

1.13 Enhanced tracking possibilities of time sheet submit and approval

Find out who and when submitted and who and when approved a time sheet super easy !

 

2. Time Management Features

2.1 Request and approve leave via Microsoft Teams

Employees can now request a leave purely via Microsoft Teams - and approvers can approve from Microsoft Teams

2.2 My calendar - Self service to display a monthly planned working time calendar

Allows a monthly calendar view on planned working time including public holidays and temporary change of workschedules. This helps rotating shift employees to find out on which day they need to work at what time.

2.3 Reporting on planned working time into the future

The existing planned working time reporting can now also include 3 months into the future

 

3. Miscellaneous

3.1 Mobile enhancements

Alerts and warnings display, conditional custom fields and custom fields for allowances in the mobile time sheet are now supported

3.2 New "Latest People Profile" as a central employee entry point for time data overview
Usability of the People Profile got enhanced tremendously and new data fields added

 

 

1. Time Tracking Features

1.0 Time Statement

Many customers in the past years regarded the unavailability of a classic time statement as a big gap in Successfactors Time. Although the actual time sheet UI provides already much more insights in time valuation results than any ESS scenario in SAP HCM Time Management and probably even more than comparable 3rd party solutions, the demand was raised to provide a classic time statement where an employee can see all recorded time data in a list for any day, plus some key informations like time account balances before and after a period, daily plus / minus values for working time accounts and of course all premium pays that the system has calculated.

And here we go - although I should be neutral I have to say that this is my highlight feature of the 2H release: a decent, proper, configurable, printable and e-mailable time statement:

The technology used for this time statement are the so-called "time forms". If you have used this in order to create letters with time data than you already know how to use this.

You can configure the time statement content to be displayed flexible. This configuration is in the end assigned to the time recording profile. With this possibility you can provide different time statements for different employee groups, like for example a time statement for positive time recorders and a time statement for negative time recorders. And the good thing is: you can re-use existing configuration ! The main configuration object is the so called "time key fact" object. And this object has already been used to configure the content for the Time Sheet Approval Center. These key facts are grouping time records or time valuation results and present them as a key fact in the Time Sheet Approval Center. And this key fact configuration is now also used for the Time Statement.

We are working with the Best Practice Team to provide a configuration template. However, this will probably only be in 1H 2025 release. 

If you want to have more detailed information on the time statement, how to configure it for example, please consult this blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/product-and-customer-updates/time-statements-empowering-employees-to-stay-informed/ba-p/13884322?emcs_t=S2h8ZW1haWx8bWVzc2FnZV9zdWJzY3JpcHRpb258TTFYWDVPU1JMVk9GMEF8MTM4ODQzMjJ8U1VCU0NSSVBUSU9OU3xoSw 

We have got a Time Statement 🙂 !!!

 

1.1 Visualization of alerts and errors in time sheet on the respective day

This here is my second most favourite feature in the new release - cause it improves end user experience tremendously. You might know that in time sheet alerts and errors are up to now only shown in a little pop-up in the footer bar. This was quite cumbersome, cause employees did not see on which exact day the alert or error has been created for. Guess what... we have changed this!!

Now you see the alerts and errors directly on day level in the weekly list overview of the time sheet! How cool is this? Small enhancement but for sure big usability improvement ! Employees see now on a glance on which day alerts or errors do exist - and even better: they can directly navigate to this day via clicking on the message !

Check out this screenshot how all this all looks like. This is now a hardcore example from one of our demo systems with alerts on any day, an error on sunday and even a very long alert text on monday. This is to show how all variations look like - and that we do not truncate any text at the end of a line if the message text is a bit longer ;-):

But what if there are more than one alert or error per day? We then only show one alert per day. There is a hard coded hierarchy that decides upon display when there is more than one alert. But usually it won´t happen often that an employee has got multiple alerts per day. For some deeper insides, this is the hierarchy: Error messages have of course a higher priority than alerts, and if there are several errors / alerts than first alerts/errors from clock in / out are shown, then time sheet errors and as a third hierarchy time sheet alerts.

And no, don´t even think on requesting to have this configurable ;-). This is a hard coded best practice hierarchy. If we would make it configurable than this just blows up implementation time and cost. Remember: a cloud solution is not an onpremise solution! Implementation, maintenance and even coding need to be cost efficient.

But what if there are indeed more than one alerts on a single day? These additional alerts are then shown in the already available message pop-up in the footer bar:

And even here we enhanced usability to a great extend: we show even in this pop-up the dates of the alert and more important: it is now a link that upon clicking opens directly on the right side the day on which the alert was raised and the employee can then very easily correct the data if needed.

Isn´t this a big usability improvement ???

And you don´t need to configure anything, it comes out of the box. So, prepare your employees that the time sheet UI is going to get better !

So, tell me in the comments if this is your 2H favourite as well - or if it is another feature. I am curios to know what you like.

 

1.2 Employee Self Service for temporary change of work schedule

I believe you are pretty familiar with our work schedule concept. The work schedule is the basis for each and everything: for absence calculation, for generating shift premiums, for overtime calculation, for working time account calculations and many more. An employees long term work schedule is assigned in the job information as part of master data. However, in many industries it can happen that there are rather ad hoc temporary changes to this long term plan. This can be relevant for only a day, a week or a bit a longer period. This is regarded as transactional data, cause only to change the master data in the job information due to the fact that an employee needs to work next week tuesday on a late shift instead of an early shift would be the wrong approach. This is why we created long time ago the temporary time information object, or in more business terms: temporary change of work schedule. These changes do not touch the job information work schedule, but just overwrite this information for the chosen period for our internal calculation purposes (and in time valuation for more sophisticated calculations both information is available.). You even can since last release overwrite the public holiday information and calendar. This is all established functionality.

However, up to now this kind of shift changes could only be done from a time administrator or a manager. But this changes now: Let me introduce our new Employee Self Service scenario for temporary change of work schedule. With this new feature an employee can request a change of his planned working time for a day or a period - and you can assign of course an approval step to this process. Let´s have a closer look:

The starting point is our new My Calendar application. The My Calendar UI shows you the planned working time of an employee in a calendar format. In the screenshot below it is a duration based employee, but of course we support akso the clock time based scenario. You find a dropdown on the right top corner that allows employees to choose a temporary change of work schedule = planned working time, or temporary change of public holiday calendar information.

In the subsequent screen you can choose the period that you want to change. After choosing the period we pre-load the workschedule information derived from the job info work schedule. This helps that you don´t need to enter something completely new from scratch. You can edit either the clocktimes, duration or change a day from a working day to a non-working day: 

 

And this is how it looks like for a clock time based employee - here you can change the start / end time of the planned working time as well as the break segments:

Changing, deleting or adding breaks can be done via clicking the link in the break column - and the net planned working time duration displayed in the UI gets then of course recalculated:

If the employee has got flextime bandwidth assigned in his normal work schedule than we display this information as well in the table - but at the moment it cannot be edited.

Important to note is: Upon choosing the period the pattern of the regular assigned workschedule gets copied into this UI. This is to improve the usability - it is much easier to adjust an entry than to create everything for the whole period from scratch. However, if you choose a 4 weeks period for example and the employee has a 1-week pattern assigned, then only this one week is shown in the table - cause it repeats for the other weeks automatically. 

Changing a work schedule might lead to retroactive changes or recalculations for time account accruals, deductions or time valuation. That´s why upon creating a temporary change of workschedule the whole internal recalculation chain gets automatically triggered so that any calculation in place gives new correct values.

It might also be that you want to restrict a bit the possibilities of employees - for example that they don´t create a temporary work schedule into the past cause this would mean a recalculation of payroll, or you want to prevent the creation of a temporary change with more than 40 hours a week, without breaks or more than x hours a day. For this kind of validations you can use save rules on this object to create some guardrails and prevent not allowing changes that are against policies. But use save rules wisely - cause they slow down performance!

But coming back to the My Calendar Ui where we started. How does an employee now see in the Calendar that a temporary change does exists? Multiple options. If a temporary change exist we display a little icon for the day. Hovering over it gives you some information and you can also see the approval status on a glance:

 

Clicking on the entry gives you details as well as: 

But we also provide the possibility to get an overview on all temporary changes via an own button in the top right corner:

I guess that´s pretty much it what can be said on this big new enhancement. Of course, we are not yet at the end of this story. This is only kind of the first shipment of enhancements on this topic. More round offs of this feature are already planned like for example the possibility to choose a day model for a day or a period. Stay tuned and watch this feature growing !

 

1.3 Employee Self Service for temporary change of public holiday calendar

Because this feature is so nice, I splitted it up into two sub-chapters ;-). If you had a close look on the screenshot above you already saw that there is not only the possibility for employees to request temporarily a planned working time change. But you could also see the button on "temporary change of public holiday calendar". This is needed when employees perform for example business trips into regions where a different public holiday calendar is valid or if they work for a short period from a different location for example. And this feature has also been included into the above mentioned Employee Self Scenario. As an employee you can now request for a day or a period the overwrite of the public holiday information as assigned to you in the job information - and of course again without the need of changing the job information:

Just as the change of planned working time you need to specify the period first and then you can either search for the relevant public holiday calendar itself, or you search for the country and it gives you all public holiday calendars for this country.

After selection you get a nice preview of the upcoming public holidays in your selected period:

Of course you can assign an approval workflow to this process. The button "temporary changes" in the upper right corner gives you an overview on your requested changes with approval status, and you can cancel and delete a record from there.

Most probably not every employee shall have the option to change his planned working time temporarily - how to prevent this? Pretty simple: we provided a new permission for this functionality. If you want to allow this you need to assign the create temporary change of holiday calendar in self-service permission and the edit permission for the object temporary time information to your employees permission roles. And to overall activate this feature you need to have of course Time Off and Time Tracking activated.

 

1.4 General availability of monthly time sheet UI and monthly submit period

Most probably you already knew from last release that we have a long awaited monthly calendar time sheet UI. You can toggle therein between a list view and the monthly calendar view:

This feature is now available for all customers. On top you can configure if the submission period (or to be precise: the "time recording approval period length" shall be a month as well. However, you can also use the monthly calendar UI with the already existing weekly approval period. The solution is now as flexible to allow both options.

 

1.5 Display of previous values in time sheet amendments

Maybe for those who are not so deep into our solution: what is a time sheet amendment? Quite easy: if a time sheet is submitted and approved it might be that an employee needs to edit once more the time sheet. He has forgotten for example some entries for past week. He needs hence to edit already approved times. And this kicks off a time sheet amendment. So this means: an amendment is a call back of an already approved time sheet, data gets edited and sent for approval again. Problem is: it could be that the payroll run has already be done - this means this is a backdated change of data that causes a recalculation of payroll. And that´s why a new approval is necessary. Of course, you can configure if an amendment is allowed or not (with our time recording admissibility object). But usually it is allowed for a couple of weeks, and what we heard from customers was, that if the amended time sheet is sent again to the approver then the approver cannot figure out on a glance which data has been changed. He just gets the whole time sheet for approval, but without before and after visualization of changed data. And this is exactly what we now provide.

This means in a nutshell:

- we show now for an amendment on the summary tab in the time sheet UI a "previous" value for time valuation results, allowances and on-call times.

- we show now for an amendment on the time accounts tab a "previous" value for our standard working time account balance and time off in lieu account balance. Let´s check out how this looks like:

Here you can see on the time sheet summary and time account block the display of previous values:

And if there are on call or allowance changes, it looks like this:

However, there are a couple of things to emphasize:

1) not for each and anything the previous values are shown. Due to the fact that we want to help in the approval process to present an approver the facts what payroll relevant data has changed compared with the already approved time sheet we decided to show those additional information only for time type groups that are configured as pay types - which means, only for the data that is send to payroll. Time account balances can be payroll relevant as well, hence the previous value is shown for those as well. However, time type groups that are only configured as UI components are not showing before values, only the after value.

2) Once the time sheet is approved - or the amendment discarded-  the previous values are not shown anymore. So this is not a replacement for an audit log! Else this would overload an end-user interface. This additional information is only intended to help managers / approvers in the approval process to understand the values that have been changed by an employee

 

1.6 Time Sheet submit attestation possibility

In some countries it is legally required to have an attestation from employees on the correctness of recorded time data. To support this process we have introduced a new configuration option to define attestation texts that come upon submitting a time sheet. These texts can be defined according to customer needs and you even can give them a variety of formatting options with the so called BB codes. Bolt text, italic text, even hyperlinks to other pages are possible. Whatever formating you want to use within the BB codes possibilities. The text length is however limited to 4000 characters. 

What does this mean concrete and how does it look like? Upon pressing the time sheet submit button you received already in the past a confirmation pop-up with comments functionality. And this one has been enhanced with the attestation text. So you can display now whatever text you want in this pop-up. And with finally clicking clicking on submit the employee has given his consent to this text. A sample text can look like this:

And is this text for all employees the same? No. Would not be smart to deliver this in that way, cause the text might vary from country to country, from employee group to employee group. That´s why the text is configured on time recording profile level. And the time recording profile is -as you know- assigned to employees in the job information. And with this we have a highly flexible mechanism.

This is how the configuration looks like in the time recording profile - it is more or less a text field that you can enrich with bb codes to do the text formatting:

And you now might ask: what about the language - when the text is maintained in english, but my employees log-on language is spanish, what then? You can see the little earth symbol on the screenshot above on the right side - upon clicking onto it you get into the translation dialogue where you can maintain different texts for different languages. Problem solved.

 

1.7 Time container display of different periods in time sheet UI

In the time sheet result section we display see time valuation results that are configured as "UI component". This is to inform employees on calculated overtime, generated night shift premiums or whatever you think is important to visualize to the employee. Just mark a calculated time type group as "UI component" and the employee can see it in the time sheet UI.

However, our new time containers can have multiple periods like bi-weekly, quarterly or a multiple of months like for example a year. See below the currently supported periods (they will be further enhanced in future): 

*1 Start must be the 1st of a month

And the new enhancement is that we even show this kind of containers in the weekly or monthly time sheet UI- if they are marked as UI-components.

Just to recap - a time container can sum up data over a period. So, each day this value changes. Let´s take a yearly overtime threshold for example. The company agreement states that an employee cannot perform more than 200 hours of overtime in a year. You can collect with time containers every overtime hour in every time sheet week and sum it up. However, as mentioned, up to now we could not display this value in the time sheet UI - and how should then an employee know how many overtime he has already performed in a year and if the threshold is close? Not possible -till now. As a new enhancement to the time sheet UI we introduced a new tab where this periodical summed up values are shown: 

You need of course mark this time container in the configuration as "UI component".  

The value shown depends on the valuation period chosen in the time valuation. If you choose "up to today" then only till the actual day the time container gets displayed. If you choose "whole sheet" then the value is calculated and displayed for the whole life-span of the time container. 

But what containers are shown in a monthly time sheet where there is an overlap to past and next month container period? On the UI you will see all time containers that overlap with the time sheet period. And this can be a weekly or a monthly time sheet. So, let´s say you have a monthly time sheet, but you want to display a weekly container to the employee. Then you will have on that tab 4 time containers with different periods - or maybe even 5 if a time container spans the month.

Furthermore any alerts or updates of values based on time containers needed in the past a manual refresh of the time sheet - and this has been overcome now as well. Time containers are calculated a bit asynchronously, with a slight delay, but you won´t really notice and updating values or triggering alerts do come as mentioned now automatically.

 

1.8 Time container integration to EC / SAP Payroll

As already described in previous blogs we have a new object called "time container" which is a kind of successor for the "time collectors". However, time container provide much more functionality in terms of supported different periods (see chapter above). You can have at the moment a multiple of weeks or months for time valuation calculation purpose.

So in the end you can have for example bi-weekly overtime calculations or even yearly overtime calculations or any other time valuation calculation. But for all of this you need the new time containers. However, there is one thing to mention for the time containers if you are not yet familiar with them: As you know everything in the time sheet and time valuation is calculated real time. Employee enters a record and automatically the time valuation is triggered and updates every result or creates alerts. However, you can imagine, with our new time periods like a yearly overtime calculation this might lead to a performance deterioration, cause I enter a record, the system needs to calculate a full year and this needs a few milliseconds. That´s why Time Containers are from an architectural point of view not really real-time, but nearly real-time :-). They have an asynchronous automated mechanism that gives you the result with a slight delay of maybe 2 seconds. 

So far so good, but what is the new feature? :-).

The new feature is that the time containers are now also replicated to Employee Central Payroll or SAP onprem Payroll Infotype 2010 renumeration info just as any other time valuation result. You need not to configure any extra for the integration, it comes out of the box. You just need to configure the time container as a "payroll time container" and of course map this to an Infotype 2010 wage type and you are done. With this new feature you can now also replicate monthly overtime calculation results or yearly or whatever other time container you have in place to EC/ SAP Payroll and get the employee paid accordingly.

If you are not running a SAP Payroll you can either use the Integration Center (see next chapter) to extract this new time containers or you can even use the corresponding public available API.

 

1.9 Time Container availability in integration center

This is a short but for many customers an important one. Time Containers are made now available for the integration center. So you can extract them and do whatever you want with them ;-). You an for example feed external payrolls or use them wherever this data is needed:

 

1.10 Time Sheet Approval Center general availability and enhancements

Last release we offered our new time sheet approval center for early adopters only. After receiving very good feedback on this UI we release it as general available. On top, it comes with some further enhancements. First a recapitulation: what is the time sheet approval center?

This new UI serves managers to quickly get detailed information for a bunch of employees to facilitate their time sheet approval process. You can configure pretty flexible key facts for the UI like for example summarized recorded allowances or overtime or any other data that helps managers to approve. You see planned time, recorded absences, pending time relevant workflows, alerts for an employee and you can quickly navigate to the time sheet and of course mass approve. This is how the UI looks like:

If you are not familiar with this new UI, please check my previous blog  as well as my PM colleague Gayathris blog on more details. I will focus here only on the enhancements.

The above screenshot shows already nearly all new enhancements: The Time Sheet Approval Center supports now also 

  • employees with time recording method "overtime" (in the screenshot Employee Oscar Moscato)
  • the french specific way of time recording in days (in the screenshot Employee Laurie LeBar where the planned and recorded column shows days instead of hours/minutes)
  • Employees with monthly time sheet period (Jose Tartaroti)
  • The information if the actual time sheet is an amendment (Jose Tartaroti)
  • and there is a new home page tile for the Time Sheet Approval Center that you can add to the homepage for quick navigation (plus a permission on it). This is how the new tile looks like on the homepage:

 

1.11 Split and delimit of temporary time information

You most probably know this functionality from absence recording: when there is an absence record in the system and you record for a full or partial overlapping period another absence record than you can configure the system in a way that an error is raised - or that a split and delimit of the existing record occurs. This makes for example sense when you are in your 3 weeks vacation and you get 1 week ill. In Germany the illness record takes over precendence on the vacation. Instead of manually reducing the vacation record you can just record overlapping the illness record and the existing vacation gets automatically split and/or delimited. 

And same functionality has now been introduced for our temporary change of work schedule (or in technical terms: the temporary time information object). If you make this configuration:

you don´t end up in an error, but in a warning when records do overlap. But you can proceed and save the new temporary change. The already existing one gets overwritten or delimited if there is only a partial overlap:

Now, you know that with this release you can attach a workflow to an temporary change of workschedule. What happens with a workflow in case of split and delimit? Handling here is just the same like for absence split and delimit: the workflow status is not changed - even not, if an existing gets delimited. There is no trigger of a new workflow for the existing and splitted (or completely overwritten) record. And with respect to workflow handling there are some specific things to be considered:

- if a workflow is attached to the temporary time information then a split and delimit is not taken place immediately upon creation, but only after approval

- it can also be the case that you have a temporary time information which is not yet approved, but you want to create overlapping a new one. However, in this case a split and delimit is not possible and ends in an error. Cause else there could be a multitude of pending data that could end simply up in a data mess.

This feature is not only a nice usability feature, but also brings massive improvement for external shift planning system that upload their shifts via API calls of the temporary time object. There is no need anymore to first delete a temporary time information before updating. You just can overwrite. So, adjust all your API calls - things are getting much more easier now ! However, even here you need to keep one thing in mind: the split and delimit works only if a new record is uploaded and hits an already existing one! If your import files contain already overlapping records than a split and delimit check in the file is of course not possible - cause which one should be splitted? So, ensure that your import file is clean.

And one last thing to add: different types of temporary change of workschedule and temporary change of public holiday calendar are not falling into split and delimit scenario - cause they can (and need to) exist in parallel. You can have from a business point of view for example a temporary change of workschedule and in the same period a temporary change of public holiday calendar. This is needed for business scenarios like business trip into a foreign country where an employee works for a specific time also with a different work schedule. 

1.12 General availability of Business rules in time valuation 

If you want to learn more on this, please consult my blog on last release features were I already introduced this feature as restricted available. I am glad that this feature is now available for all customers, cause this will ease your time valuation configuration, reduce the number of time recording profiles needed and overall give you much more flexibility in the time valuation.

I am just give a short overview on this feature now - consult my last blog to have more details. 

You have now the possibility to use business rules in time valuation. We provide two new rule scenarios and accordingly two new valuation types that "call" these rule scenarios. 

Here you can see the new rule scenarios:

And the new time valuation types that trigger this rules are:

- Decision by Business Rule

- Calculation by Business Rule

The overall beauty and flexibility of the business rule is that you can work with if / else statements and this makes your configuration much more easier. On top, they are effective dated. So you can for example add a new time slice if a company agreement on an overtime threshold will change on 1st January from 40 hours to 38.5 hours for example.

However, and this is the disclaimer, don´t regard the business rules in time valuation as an all-mighty weapon. As mentioned already in several blogs, business rules are slow in performance and that´s why the time valuation engine comes with their own "rules" in order to be able to perform a real time valuation. Business rules are a complete different technology than time valuation rules. Hence they need to be wisely incorporated into the time valuation. We established a mechanism that kills long lasting processes in order to prevent bad neighbour effects to other customers running on the same data center. Test your rules properly in the test instances to avoid this in production! This is also why the number of allowed business rules per time recording profile is limited to only 5 at the moment. And be aware that the fields available in the rules are at the moment limited and that the rule functions provided are reduced. This means not each and any use case is supported. You anyway need to check if your use case is not better covered with existing time valuation types - cause misusing a business rule for something that can be covered with normal time valuation leads for sure to a deterioration of performance. So, check out the available fields and rule functions for those two rule scenarios - you will see that these can at the moment not be compared to the business rules possibilities you might know from Time Off business rule scenarios. And most important: think on the solution approach ! Can same result not be achieved with existing time valuations?

To nail it: the job information and internal time valuation fields that can currently be queried are:

  • Hire Date (TimeValuationCalculationRuleRequest.jobInfo.employmentInfo.start_date)
  • Employment Type (TimeValuationCalculationRuleRequest.jobInfo.employmentInfo.assignment_type)
  • Original Start Date (TimeValuationCalculationRuleRequest.jobInfo.employmentInfo.originalStartDate)
  • Date of Birth (TimeValuationCalculationRuleRequest.jobInfo.employmentInfo.personInfo.date_of_birth)
  • Pay Grade (TimeValuationCalculationRuleRequest.jobInfo.pay_grade)
  • Is Fulltime Employee (TimeValuationCalculationRuleRequest.jobInfo.is_fulltime_employee)
  • FTE (TimeValuationCalculationRuleRequest.jobInfo.fte)
  • Location (TimeValuationCalculationRuleRequest.jobInfo.location)
  • Job Classification (TimeValuationCalculationRuleRequest.jobInfo.job_code)
  • Employee Class (TimeValuationCalculationRuleRequest.jobInfo.employee_class)
  • Department (TimeValuationCalculationRuleRequest.jobInfo.department)
  • Logical Date (TimeValuationCalculationRuleRequest.logicalDate)
  • Input Value (TimeValuationCalculationRuleRequest.sumOfInputGroupsInMinutes)
  • Time Valuation Method (TimeValuationCalculationRuleRequest.timeValuationMethod)

And the available rule functions at the moment:

  • Add()
  • Minus()
  • Multiply()
  • Divide()
  • Modulo()
  • DatePlus()
  • Round()
  • Difference In Calendar Years()
  • Difference In Years Round Down()
  • Difference In Years Round Up()
  • Get First Day Of Month() Can be used to get first and last of month (with DatePlus())
  • TreatNullAs()

This is it. Might be that in future those get enhanced to increase flexibility and functionality.

Now lets have a closer look onto the decision rule capabilities:

Decision rules allow to query some Job Information fields and based on that "decide" to route the input time type group either to the time type group above or below. Looks schematically like this:

 

If you want to use the planned working time in time valuation depending on the Full time equivalent for example you can use a time valuation of type "decision by business rules", trigger a business rule that queries the FTE from Job Information. And if FTE is 1 you move the input time type group into the above time type group, if less than 1 - which means part time employee - into the below time type group. The time valuation rule would look like this:

And the corresponding business rule that is triggered would look like this:

The input time type group contains in this example the planned working time - but you can of course choose whatever time type group you need. The main message is: you can route a time type group depending on Job Information (even effective dated) into different time type groups and use them for any further time valuation steps.

And of course - whenever the job information fields are updated retroactively a recalculation of this time valuation is triggered. 

The second rule scenario we deliver is the "Time Valuation Calculation" business rule scenario.

Schematically:

This time valuation type calls a business rules in which you can do some basic mathematical calculations and create a value that you then can use in subsequent time valuations.

You have got for example a yearly overtime threshold of 200 hours. But for an employee hired on 15st November you don´t want to have 200 hours, but a prorated threshold based on the hire date. You can use a time valuation of type "Calculation by Business rules" which calls for example a business rules similar to this:

The calculation rule can also be used to "generate" a value out of nothing. Let´s have a look on this time valuation rule that calls a calculation rule. And note: there is no input time type:

In the corresponding business rule the value is set to 60 minutes:

And remember: due to the effective dating of business rules you can set a different value effective of whatever date you want.

You can apply a daily or period valuation and if you choose daily than 1 hour gets posted each day to the time to group above. This works for clock time or duration time recording method, however, if clock time than "fake" clock times are added starting from 00:00 as long as the duration you have specified. This is necessary cause any time type group for clock time recorders need to have internally clock times.

If you choose period valuation then one value is calculated for the whole period, means 1 hour per week or month. And the result is shifted always to the last day of the period.

This is it. A first step into probably a new world of flexibility and functionality. Stay tuned and hopefully watch this feature grow.

 

1.13 Enhanced tracking possibilities of time sheet submit and approval

Up to now it was only possible to find out who submitted and approved a time sheet at what date / time via our audit reports. We provide now a more user friendly possibility: we enhanced time sheet object with 4 new fields so that you can use them in a normal report. The fields are:

- submitted by: lets you track the user who submitted the time sheet

- submission date: lets you track the date when the time sheet was submitted

- approved by: let´s you track the user who approved the time sheet last

- approval date: let´s you track the date on when the time sheet was approved

And of course this fields can now be added to the normal reporting stories, just like in this example:

And of course - due to the fact that this fields are added to the object they are available via managed data as well for example as a filter/search possibility.

And with this you can easily find out who and when submitted as well as approved a time sheet - in a nice report or for admins in ad hoc actions like checking the object via manage data.

 

2. Time Management Features

2.1 Request and approve leave via Microsoft Teams

This is a small but very handsome feature that simplifies the leave request process. Many of us office workers use MS Teams application as a kind of normal business communication channel. Before writing a mails a chat question is much more faster fired, hence this tool is open and used all day long from many. Imagine you could use this now to request your leave. How cool is this? Now, the MS Teams add-in has been enhanced to cover exactly this use case. You just can enter a prompt to get the leave request form:

and after this you can just choose the absence type, period and even upload an attachment as you can do in the original request form:

Custom fields, validations everything works like in the original request form.

You can also launch the application from the Teams overview page:

And on top leave request approvals can also be done from MS Teams:

All in all a very good possibility to request or approve leave whilst using the teams application.

2.2 My calendar - Self Service to display monthly planned working time calendar

This new UI was already briefly explained in the chapter on the Self Service for temporary change of workschedule. We provide a new monthly planned working time calendar where employees can see their daily planned time and public holiday information. This UI is intended to be enhanced in the future with more team calendar capabilities.

 

2.3 Reporting on planned working time into the future

Last release we provided the possibility to report on planned working time. Check for more details my last release blog.

However, it was not possible to use this report to get planned working time data for a future date, reporting was only possible into past dates. This has now changed.

In the Time Management Configuration object, we've introduced two new fields:

- Generate Planned Working Time Data for Past Period (in months)
- Generate Planned Working Time Data for Future Period (in months).

These fields allow you to generate planned working time data in the past for up to 12 months and 3 months into the future. 

 

3. Miscellaneous

 3.1 Mobile Time Sheet enhancements

Let me just give a quick snapshot on the enhancements for mobile time sheet delivered by our mobile engineering team:

  • Alerts and warnings are displayed in the mobile time sheet
  • Support of conditional custom fields in mobile time sheet
  • Support of custom fields for allowances in mobile time sheet

For more details and screenshots please refer to the blog of my PM Mobile colleague Akshay.

 

3.2 New "Latest People Profile" as a central employee entry point for time data overview

As you probably know: a new "latest people profile" has been shipped. And in the time management section this brings a couple of new enhancements. All relevant time information is consolidated now at one central place. Plus, compared to the old people profile, there are significant usability improvements:

- time account balances (instead of time type balances) are shown as well as the booking end dates of the time accounts

- public holidays in addition to absences

- time sheet summary card indicating if action needs to be taken, for example if recordings on days are missing and how many hours are missing. Time recording method like positive, negative or overtime recording is of course considered here

- the time relevant job information fields are shown in the time section so that all necessary information is at one place

And this is how the full profile of the latest people profile looks like:

 

This is it again. Or better: this was it. My latest and also last blog. This time I cannot end with the usual phrase: "stay tuned" for my next blog. There will of course in the next years much more really cool new features (I can tell, cause I know already the planning details 😉 ), however, at least I won´t deliver any insights in this anymore. Again, thanks for accompanying me and the product evolution during the last 10 years. All the best for the future, I wish you many successful Time Tracking implementations !

Volker Ruof

entry.title: 2H 2024 Release Highlights : SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Updates to Watch Out For
entry.content:
As we transition into the second half of 2024, SAP SuccessFactors has rolled out several exciting updates to Employee Central. These enhancements are designed to make your HR processes smoother, more intuitive, and ultimately more empowering for both employees and administrators. Let’s dive into the most noteworthy changes, from streamlined workflows to enhanced employee experience features, and how they can benefit your organization.

 

1. Access Company Structure Overview Using Deep Link

Accessing your company’s structure is now easier than ever. With the new deep links, you can jump straight to the Company Structure Overview page with just a click. Whether you need to check departmental structures or specific roles, this feature allows quick access with sharable, bookmarked URLs—saving time for you and your team.

Here's an example of using a deep link: https://<app-server-domain>/sf/orgchart?type=entity&bplte_company=ExampleCompany&objectType=Department&evaluationPath=13198002&instance=123956

 

2. Access Pending Approvals and Future Changes in Latest People Profile

The People Profile just got a productivity boost! Pending approvals and future changes can now be accessed directly from the profile, helping you manage tasks right where you need them. Whether it’s a promotion waiting for approval or a job title update, you’ll find it all in one place, ready for action.

 

3. Access the Benefits Master Data Job Through Scheduled Job Manager

Managing benefits enrollment just became more user-friendly. Administrators can now handle Benefit Enrollment jobs directly from the Scheduled Job Manager, aligning with other benefits-related tasks. This addition simplifies job configuration and management, bringing consistency across the board.

 

4. Multi-Currency Support in Benefits Enrollment

For companies with global teams, the multi-currency support feature in Benefits Enrollment is a game changer. Employees can now select benefits in multiple currencies during the enrollment process, making it easier for global employees to understand the true cost and value of their benefits. Until now, an employee could only use benefits with the same currency. This feature helps those employees who are eligible for benefits with multiple currencies.

 

5. New Benefits Enrollment Action Card on the Home Page

Forget missed deadlines! The Benefits Enrollment Action Card is a gentle reminder on your home page, guiding you to complete enrollment steps. It shows the due dates for open enrollment, so employees can stay on top of important tasks.

 

6. Enhanced Higher Duty and Temporary Assignment Options

With expanded support for Delegation Only temporary assignments, administrators can now configure these assignments more flexibly, allowing organizations to handle workforce needs more effectively.

 

7. Multiple Employee Central Instances for Seamless S/4HANA Integration

For companies with complex organizational structures, SAP SuccessFactors now enables multiple Employee Central instances to connect to a single SAP S/4HANA client. This integration simplifies data management across global branches, enabling a unified view of HR and organizational data.

SAP S/4HANA system must have the following minimum stack level:

  • SAP S/4HANA 2022: Support Package Stack 4 or higher
  • SAP S/4HANA 2023: Feature Package Stack 2 or higher

 

8. New Permissions for Contingent Workforce Management

Managing contingent workers is now more secure with the enhanced Add Assignment permission. Only authorized users will be able to create concurrent assignments, ensuring that contingent workforce data is handled responsibly. If you don't enable this new permission setting, then the Add Assignment option won't appear under the Take Action dropdown menu in People Profile.

 

9. Employee Central Quick Actions Just Got Better

The Quick Actions menu has been refined to help you access and update key employee data efficiently. For instance, the Incumbent of Parent Position field is now readily accessible, making it easier to manage transfers and promotions.

 

10. Enhanced Business Configuration with New Validations

The latest release brings new validations to Business Configuration for country-specific fields. These validations help maintain data consistency, ensuring your organization’s data meets global standards.

Changes include:

  • isPrimary now mandatory
  • Keeping "default"' as data type for country fields - Changing these fields to other types, such as Picklist, is now restricted to prevent data discrepancies
  • Removal of alternative fields in country/region-specific address elements - Alternative fields end with "-alt1" or "-alt2". They're now unavailable from the dropdown list when users add a new field in a country/region-specific element. When editing and saving an existing country/region-specific element with alternative fields, they're prompted to delete these fields first.

11. My Team Positions: A Better View for Managers

Managers will appreciate the added details on the My Team Positions page, which now shows the FTE value and "To Be Hired" status. These updates give managers a more accurate view of staffing needs and team structures.

 

12. My Calendar UI as Employee Self-Service

The new monthly calendar UI helps employees to get an overview of their planned working times, including temporary changes and holidays. SAP made this enhancement so that employees can get an overview of their working times easily. Before this enhancement, it was not possible for employees to get a monthly overview of their planned working times in a single calendar view.

 

13. Updates to Pending Workflows Page

The new Pending Workflows page in SAP SuccessFactors has been enhanced and now fully replaces the legacy Pending Requests page, offering improved functionality. Key updates include a new tab, “Requests Reassigned to Others,” which helps users track requests they’ve delegated or escalated. This feature will also be available on the SAP SuccessFactors Mobile app with the 2H 2024 Production release. Users will find an easy link to access requests awaiting their approval and enhanced navigation, allowing them to view workflows on the Requests for Information tab, directing them to relevant pages like People Profile or Manage Positions. With these updates, the option to revert to the old Pending Requests UI has been removed, streamlining user experience in managing and responding to pending requests efficiently.

 

Conclusion: SAP SuccessFactors is Here to Make Your Work Easier

These 2H 2024 updates emphasize SAP’s commitment to a user-centered experience, from more intuitive navigation and enhanced configurability to improved access to information. With these enhancements, SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central is more than just an HR tool—it’s a comprehensive platform for employee empowerment, administrative efficiency, and organizational growth.

Ready to experience the new features? Dive into your SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central and discover how these updates can make a difference in your daily workflows!

entry.title: How SAP manages its Early Talent programs – Get insights by joining our CEI project
entry.content:
SAPs Vocational Training (VT) department, responsible for training SAP’s apprentices and dual students, uses SAP SuccessFactors for its processes. However, there are apprentice and student specific processes not covered by SAP SuccessFactors, for which the VT department used custom solutions and many spreadsheets in the past. This caused redundancies, data flow gaps, a lack of transparency and a resource intensive reporting.

As dual students & apprentices ourselves, we wanted to solve those challenges and make our own lives easier, by developing a solution covering the unique requirements for early talent management and connecting it with the data from SAP SuccessFactors, providing a unified overview for students, trainers and managers alike.

Our internal Early Talent Management solution is built from the ground up on SAP Business Technology Platform, tightly integrated into SAP SuccessFactors, with now over 20 Fiori applications, covering most of the requirements of SAPs 14 VT programs from around the globe. We use the solution at SAP since mid 2022 and are currently evaluating if it could also be used to manage our interns.

With this Customer Engagement Initiative we’d like to share insights about the capabilities of our solution and evaluate if our internal solution might also be suitable for customer use cases in a potential future solution.

Form of activities

The final form of activities depends on the participants preferences. We currently plan:

  • Initial call (1h) in December 2024 to give an initial overview about the solutions capabilities as well as the timeline of the CEI project.
  • Throughout January till June 2025 deep dives (1-2h) into specific capability areas of the solution, including time for Q&A and feedback as well as discussing challenges you currently face. Our capability areas include:
    • Student & Cohort management
    • Work (Rotation) assignment & management
    • Conversion process for students into full-time positions
    • Academics & grade reporting by students
    • Extracurricular activities for students
    • Scientific papers & a small paper book library system
  • Furthermore, we’d like to offer two sessions (1-2h) focused on your current challenges in the following two areas to get a more holistic understanding of them:
    • Apprentice handling including the Apprentice report book (Berichtsheft)
    • Functionalities required for managing interns
  • Closing call (1h) in Q3 2025

Prerequisites

Customers and partners should use or plan to use SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central and be familiar with apprenticeships, internships, co-op student or Vocational Training programs

Register for our CEI project ‘SAP Early Talent Management’ from October 14th till November 15th, 2024.

Once the registration process is finalized, we invite participants to the initial call to go through the project scope and timelines.

We are looking forward to meet you in December. 🙂

entry.title: Understanding Why Only Key Articles of Saudi Labor Law – 2024 Changes – are Addressed
entry.content:
As we announced back in August 2024 about 2024 Labor Law Changes, the Saudi Labor Law underwent significant updates with nearly 50 articles being revised (exactly 47). However, the announcement as well as the published solution (KBA 3532505) both focus primarily on only 10 articles - which is just 25% - that directly impact key HR, Time, and Payroll processes in SAP products, particularly in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central, Employee Central Time-Off, and Employee Central Payroll. This prioritization reflects a careful analysis of the articles and their direct effect on employer’s day-to-day operations. Let’s break down the reasons behind this focus.

Direct Impact on HR and Payroll Systems

The 10 articles highlighted in the KBA (37, 40, 53, 72, 74, 75, 79, 107, 113, and 151) have immediate implications for payroll calculations, employee master data, time management, and statutory reporting. These articles demand configuration changes, updates to payroll schemas, or enhanced validation checks in HR systems. For example, adjustments related to overtime calculations, end of service benefits, and wage deductions directly align with existing functionalities in payroll and time management systems.

Labor Law Definitions & Exceptions
