Articles 2 and 7 primarily provide general legal definitions and exceptions, which don’t directly impact the core HR and payroll system configurations. These sections are more context-driven, ensuring that employers understand the overarching legal framework rather than introducing technical requirements for SAP systems.

National Recruiting Portal and Recruiting Agencies

Articles 30 and 31 discuss the role of recruiting agencies and their regulations. These relationships, much like the National Recruiting Portal (Articles 22, 23, 24 & 27), are external to the employer’s HR and payroll systems, often managed by third-party platforms or external tools rather than integrated into SAP solutions.

Required Data from Employers

Articles 25 and 28 mandate that employers provide specific data, yet they don’t define fixed reporting formats. Employers have the flexibility to report using their preferred format, which SAP’s standard reporting tools can easily accommodate. The KBA does not delve deeply into these articles as the SAP solutions already cover such customizable reporting needs.

Compliance, Inspection, and Penalties

Articles 35, 39, 61, and 195-199, 203, 205-209 and 229, 230 discuss compliance with labor laws and regulations, along with the importance of inspections by the labor authority and potential penalties for non-compliance. While critical, these articles do not require system changes within HR or payroll modules, as they mostly emphasize adherence to existing laws. Compliance tracking can be supported via standard SAP HR and GRC solutions, but there are no direct impacts on payroll processing.

Training & Qualification

Articles 42-44 and 46-48 discuss requirements for training and professional development. These sections mainly apply to talent management, which falls outside the scope of localization for payroll and time management. However, employers using SAP SuccessFactors Learning can align their systems with these regulations if desired.

Employment Contract Template

Articles 51 and 52 specify the guidelines for creating employment contracts. While a template is provided, employers are free to customize it to fit their needs. SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting allows organizations to handle contract customization easily without the need for legislative-driven changes in HR or payroll modules.

Industry-Specific Regulations: Marine Work

Articles 168, 178, and 182 set specific employment conditions for workers in the marine industry. These high-level rules provide guidelines specific to this industry but do not directly impact the configuration of payroll or HR systems, as they apply primarily to employment practices in specialized sectors.

Conclusion

The decision to focus on 10 core articles in the KBA is driven by the direct impact these articles have on SAP HR and payroll processes. While the law introduces widespread changes, the remaining articles either provide contextual guidelines, industry-specific rules, or emphasize compliance that doesn’t directly affect SAP system configurations. By prioritizing the most relevant articles, the KBA enables organizations to quickly adapt to the most urgent and impactful changes while leaving flexibility for handling broader, less system-specific legal changes.

entry.title: Second Half 2024 Release: What’s New in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central?
entry.content:
The Second Half 2024 Release delivers many exciting innovations and enhancements within SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central. We are introducing new capabilities designed to enhance experiences and boost productivity for all users. Here is a quick snapshot of what you can expect to see in this release:

Refreshed People Profile
Building upon the People Profile innovations in our First Half 2024 Release, we are excited to unveil a refreshed look and feel for the full profile view. This modern design, available on desktop and mobile devices, features an intuitive card-style format that makes finding information quick and easy. And with new detailed views, users can navigate to comprehensive summaries of key profile sections, such as compensation information.

Refreshed People Profile (Full View)

AI-Assisted Writing
Additionally, employees can now use our AI-assisted writing functionality to quickly enhance their self-description in the Profile Preview, allowing them to further build their personal brand. Managers and admins can also use AI to create more meaningful workflow comments, helping speed up the approval process.

AI-Assisted Writing for Self-Description

Position Budgeting Control Workbench
We are also excited to introduce a Position Budgeting Control workbench that provides public services organizations with a streamlined view into employee-financing related activities. This new intuitive experience enables simplified error handling and helps ensure commitment runs are executed efficiently and accurately.

Position Budgeting Control Workbench

These are just a few of the many innovations in the SAP SuccessFactors Second Half 2024 Release. To learn more, check out the resources below:

  • Visit the SAP SuccessFactors Community for release documentation, product videos, and solution area blogs
  • Watch the 2H 2024 Release Highlights Video, featuring Bianka Woelke, GVP Head of Application Product Management

entry.title: Implementing SAP SuccessFactors for 200+ Legal Entities: A Global Transformation Journey
entry.content:
Implementing SAP SuccessFactors Across 200+ Legal Entities: A Comprehensive Solution for Global Operations

In today’s fast-paced business environment, it is crucial for organizations to have a robust Human Resources (HR) management system that aligns with global operations, supports local compliance, and enhances employee experience. As part of our strategic transformation, we successfully implemented SAP SuccessFactors (SF) for over 200 legal entities, utilizing a unified SAP SuccessFactors instance that extends across all affiliates. This integration is a game-changer for our global HR operations and is increasingly becoming a trend among SAP SF customers.

One SAP SuccessFactors Instance for All Affiliates

A significant advantage of our implementation is the use of a single SAP SuccessFactors instance that serves all 200+ affiliates. This is a relatively rare approach among SAP SF customers and sets a high benchmark for global HR management. By using one unified system, we align all affiliates under a common HR platform, ensuring consistent processes, unified data management, and seamless reporting. This structure not only streamlines HR operations but also offers centralized control while maintaining flexibility for local customization.

Aligning with Group Affiliates, Company Policies, and Procedures

Our implementation is designed to align with the corporate structure of the group, ensuring compliance with group-wide policies and procedures while still catering to the unique needs of each affiliate. The SAP SuccessFactors platform provides the flexibility to create global templates and country-specific configurations, making it easier to manage diverse HR practices across regions. This ensures that all legal entities adhere to the same standards, with room for local adaptations, ensuring a balance between global consistency and local compliance.

Diversity and Regional Customization: NA, EMEA, APAC, Japan, China

Given the global nature of our business, the diversity of regions such as North America (NA), Europe, Middle East, and Africa (EMEA), Asia Pacific (APAC), Japan, and China presents unique challenges and opportunities. SAP SuccessFactors allows us to address regional and cultural differences by customizing the system to meet the specific needs of each region, whether it's language preferences, legal requirements, or country-specific HR practices.

  • Global Template: The global template provides the foundation for all affiliates, ensuring consistency in HR processes such as employee management, talent acquisition, performance evaluation, and learning & development.
  • Country-Specific Templates: To address local legal requirements and cultural differences, we have implemented country-specific templates for each affiliate. This ensures compliance with country-specific labor laws, taxation rules, and employment regulations, while maintaining global process consistency.

Single Sign-On (SSO) for All Affiliates with 15 IDPs

To simplify access for all users across 200+ affiliates, we’ve implemented Single Sign-On (SSO) for all users across the organization. With SSO integrated with 15 Identity Providers (IDPs), employees can access the SAP SuccessFactors platform using their corporate credentials. This enhances security, reduces login friction, and provides a seamless user experience. The integration with SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS) ensures centralized user management and security policies.

Role-Based Permissions (RBP): Global and Company-Specific Configurations

Role-Based Permissions (RBP) is critical to ensuring that the right individuals have the appropriate access to HR data, based on their roles within the organization. In our SAP SuccessFactors instance, RBP is configured both globally and for specific affiliates to ensure compliance with company policies and data privacy regulations.

  • Global RBP: Global role-based access controls ensure that HR data can be managed centrally while adhering to global HR policies.
  • Company-Specific RBP: Specific configurations allow for tailored permissions, ensuring that data access is aligned with local roles, business units, and organizational hierarchies.

Data Privacy Compliance: GDPR and PIPL Laws

Compliance with data privacy regulations is one of the most significant challenges when managing a global workforce. With employees spread across the globe, our SAP SuccessFactors implementation ensures adherence to critical regulations like GDPR and China's Personal Information Protection Law (PIPL).

  • GDPR Compliance for EMEA: In line with GDPR regulations, we have implemented specific restrictions for affiliates in the EMEA region, ensuring that personal data is handled according to local data privacy laws. SAP SuccessFactors provides robust data protection features, allowing us to restrict data access, implement consent management, and manage data retention policies to meet GDPR requirements.
  • China PIPL Compliance: Similarly, for our affiliates in China, we have configured the system to ensure compliance with the China PIPL law. This includes the proper handling of sensitive data and ensuring that employees' personal information is stored and processed in accordance with Chinese data protection laws.

Integration with SAP HCM for 10 Affiliates

Our integration with SAP Human Capital Management (HCM) is a critical aspect of the system, allowing us to synchronize data between SAP SuccessFactors and other legacy systems. This integration ensures a seamless flow of HR data, including employee information, compensation details, and organizational structure, across various SAP HCM modules. Currently, we have integrated SAP SuccessFactors with SAP HCM for 10 affiliates, enabling smooth data exchange and enhancing the accuracy of HR data across the organization.

Interfaces for 100+ Affiliates

In addition to the integration with SAP HCM, we have established interfaces for more than 100 affiliates, ensuring that data from these entities is synchronized with the global SAP SuccessFactors platform. These interfaces support various HR processes such as payroll, performance reviews, and training records, ensuring that all affiliates remain aligned with the global HR strategy.

Integration with SAP SuccessFactors Submodules

We have also integrated various SAP SuccessFactors submodules with our global HR platform, including:

  • Recruiting: For seamless talent acquisition and candidate management.
  • Learning: For tracking and managing employee learning and development.
  • Performance & Goals: To streamline performance evaluations and goal setting across affiliates.
  • Compensation: For managing compensation structures and ensuring pay equity across regions.

These integrations provide a holistic view of employee data and streamline HR processes, enhancing the overall HR experience for both employees and managers.

Reporting: ADHOC, CANVAS, and Story Reports

To support decision-making and performance tracking, we have implemented various reporting tools within SAP SuccessFactors:

  • ADHOC Reports: These allow HR leaders to generate custom reports based on real-time data, empowering them to make informed decisions.
  • CANVAS Reports: SAP SuccessFactors’ CANVAS reporting tool allows for visually rich, interactive reports that are easily customizable to meet the needs of different stakeholders.
  • Story Reports: Story reports offer a dynamic and narrative-driven way to analyze HR data, providing insights that help guide strategic planning and performance evaluations.

Conclusion

By implementing SAP SuccessFactors across 200+ legal entities, we have established a global HR platform that is flexible, compliant with regional regulations, and fully aligned with our organizational goals. This comprehensive system supports everything from global talent management to country-specific HR practices, making it a powerful tool for enhancing collaboration, streamlining workflows, and driving organizational success. The integration of advanced features like SSO, RBP, GDPR, and PIPL compliance, along with robust reporting tools, positions our organization to manage HR operations efficiently and in full compliance with global data privacy standards. As we continue to evolve and expand, SAP SuccessFactors remains a key enabler of our HR transformation journey.

entry.title: Why Companies Moved Out of SAP SuccessFactors and How to Retain Them: A Strategic Approach
entry.content:
Why Companies Moved Out of SAP SuccessFactors: Retaining SAP SuccessFactors Clients and Improving Support

SAP SuccessFactors (SF) has been one of the leading cloud-based HR solutions globally, enabling companies to streamline their HR processes, manage talent, and align their workforce with organizational goals. However, over the past few years, several companies have decided to move away from SAP SuccessFactors after using it for 5 to 10 years. This trend raises questions about why these companies are leaving and what SAP can do to retain them. In this blog, we will explore the reasons behind companies moving out of SAP SuccessFactors, SAP’s strategy for retaining clients, and how businesses can offer better support to their customers.

Why Did Companies Move Out of SAP SuccessFactors?

There are several reasons why some organizations have chosen to transition away from SAP SuccessFactors:

  1. Cost Considerations

As organizations scale or grow, the total cost of ownership (TCO) of SAP SuccessFactors can increase significantly. Some companies find that the cost of SAP’s licenses, maintenance fees, and additional modules can outweigh the benefits they are receiving, especially if they are not fully utilizing all the platform's capabilities.

Example: A mid-sized company might have initially opted for SAP SuccessFactors for its comprehensive modules but later realized that it only needed a basic HR solution, prompting them to switch to a less expensive alternative.

  1. Complexity and Usability Issues

While SAP SuccessFactors offers a broad range of features, some users find it complex and difficult to navigate. The system’s interface, although rich in functionality, can sometimes be unintuitive, leading to user frustration and decreased engagement. Organizations that want simpler, more user-friendly systems may turn to competitors like Workday or BambooHR, which offer more streamlined interfaces.

Example: Employees at an organization could be experiencing slow adoption rates due to the learning curve, which may lead to inefficiencies, prompting them to explore easier-to-use HR platforms.

  1. Customization and Flexibility Concerns

SAP SuccessFactors provides a high level of customization, but in some cases, it may not fully meet the unique needs of certain organizations. For instance, small to mid-sized companies with specific industry requirements may find SAP’s system rigid, making it difficult to tailor the software to their needs.

Example: A growing tech startup might need faster integrations with other tools that are easier to implement in other systems like BambooHR or Oracle HCM Cloud, which may be more agile.

  1. Integration Challenges

While SAP SuccessFactors offers strong integration capabilities, some companies experience challenges when connecting SAP SuccessFactors with their other legacy systems or third-party applications. This can lead to data silos and inefficiencies, causing some organizations to switch to other HR platforms that offer more seamless integration with their ecosystem.

Example: A global organization using SAP HCM may find it difficult to integrate SuccessFactors with regional payroll systems, prompting a migration to a platform with more out-of-the-box integrations.

  1. Customer Support and Response Time

Some companies have reported dissatisfaction with SAP’s customer support, particularly regarding timely responses and effective resolution of issues. In a rapidly changing business environment, organizations need quick and effective support to avoid disruptions. Poor customer support can drive companies to look for alternatives that provide more responsive service.

Example: A company experiencing repeated system downtimes might seek out a competitor offering 24/7 support and faster response times.

How Can SAP Retain These Companies?

SAP has a strong product in SuccessFactors, but to retain customers, it needs to address the concerns that drive organizations to seek alternatives. Here are some strategies SAP can adopt:

  1. Reduce Complexity and Improve User Experience

SAP should focus on simplifying its platform to enhance the user experience. By streamlining the user interface and making it more intuitive, companies can improve engagement and adoption rates. Additionally, offering personalized onboarding and training can help companies get more value from the system.

How to Implement: SAP can create more simplified, industry-specific templates or set up dedicated teams to provide personalized training based on organizational needs.

  1. Lower the Total Cost of Ownership (TCO)

Offering flexible pricing models and transparent licensing can help reduce the overall cost for organizations, making SAP SuccessFactors more accessible for a wider range of companies, especially small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs).

How to Implement: SAP can introduce tiered pricing models or usage-based pricing to allow companies to scale their usage of SuccessFactors as their needs grow.

  1. Enhance Integration Capabilities

SAP should focus on improving the ease and speed of integration with other third-party applications, as well as enhancing its API capabilities. Companies are looking for solutions that can be seamlessly integrated with existing systems and workflows.

How to Implement: SAP can expand its pre-built connectors for popular platforms or enhance its integration hub to simplify the integration process with external systems.

  1. Improve Customer Support and Service

Providing more responsive, proactive, and personalized customer support can go a long way in improving customer satisfaction. SAP should ensure that customers can get timely assistance when facing issues and provide robust self-help resources, including comprehensive knowledge bases and community forums.

How to Implement: SAP can offer dedicated account managers for key clients, as well as quicker access to support agents via live chat or phone support.

  1. Drive Continuous Innovation

Continuous improvement and new features are key to retaining customers. SAP SuccessFactors needs to keep up with changing HR trends, such as AI-driven HR analytics, mobile accessibility, and enhanced employee experience functionalities, to stay competitive in the HR tech space.

How to Implement: SAP should focus on integrating cutting-edge technologies like machine learning and AI to offer predictive analytics and automation for HR tasks.

How Can Businesses Offer Better Support to SAP SuccessFactors Clients?

To better support clients and ensure long-term satisfaction, businesses can:

  • Provide Dedicated Support Teams: Offer specialized support for each client, particularly those with unique configurations or specific needs.
  • Invest in User Training: Ensure that clients have ongoing training and resources to get the most out of SAP SuccessFactors.
  • Conduct Regular Check-Ins: Set up regular meetings to assess the client’s needs, address concerns, and propose potential improvements to their system.

Conclusion:

SAP SuccessFactors remains a powerful tool for HR transformation, but to retain customers, SAP must evolve based on customer needs, focusing on user experience, cost-efficiency, integration capabilities, and customer support. By addressing the reasons why companies are leaving and offering solutions tailored to their requirements, SAP can keep its clients satisfied and help them succeed in a dynamic business environment. Businesses supporting SAP SuccessFactors clients should ensure they are providing top-notch support, personalized service, and continuous improvements to keep customers engaged.

We welcome your comments and suggestions on how to retain SAP SuccessFactors customers and continue to provide them with a valuable business solution.

entry.title: Second Half 2024 Release: What's New in SAP SuccessFactors Time Management
entry.content:
It’s time to cover the 2024 second half SAP SuccessFactors Time Management release. In this blog, I’ll highlight key features from the 2H 2024 release, now available in preview and coming to production instances on November 15th. For full details, be sure to check out the 'What's New Viewer'.

Improving the time management experience for employees.

This release brings new capabilities for a more seamless employee and manager experience. Employees can now request time off directly within Microsoft Teams, simplifying the process by allowing employees to stay within the app they use most. The experience mirrors that within SAP SuccessFactors, maintaining data validations and alerts. Managers can also approve time-off requests in Teams, and employees can sync upcoming leave with their Microsoft Teams Calendar.


Request time off via Microsoft Teams​

Employees can use new calendar features in SAP SuccessFactors to manage their schedules more flexibly, enhancing work-life balance. They can self-propose their work schedule, reducing work for managers and HR. They can also request a temporary shift change, and even reschedule public holidays for future use in case of business travel or missed holidays. Validation rules help ensure compliance with contractual agreements, and built-in approval workflow ensures managers are informed and can approve requests.

Employee self-service work schedule change

Additionally, employees can now view time valuation warnings and errors directly in their mobile time sheet, making adjustments on the go. New custom fields for allowances also help improve compliance, which leads me to the next section of this blog.

Mobile alerting

Ensuring compliance and payroll accuracy.

In the last release, we introduced the Time Sheet Approval Center, designed to be the one-stop shop for managers to monitor and process time data for employees. This has been the most successful Restricted Availability program we’ve run to date. This 2H 2024 release makes it General Available and introduces new enhancements to help managers ensure HR regulatory compliance and minimize payroll errors. Amendments highlight any changes made by employees after a time sheet is approved and resubmitted, so managers can see all information at a glance. This makes it easier for managers to track changes and approve time sheets with confidence. Additionally, the mass approval capability boosts efficiency, helping managers handle approvals swiftly.  

Time Sheet Approval Center

We are also introducing a new time statement to help ensure compliance with HR regulations that require the publication of this information. This feature is particularly important in, but not limited to, Austria, Germany and Switzerland. The time statement helps employees quickly understand their time valuation results, including planned and worked hours, overtime, and absences. The templates are fully configurable and can be defined based on the employee recording profile. The statements can be emailed or saved as a PDF, which makes it widely accessible for all employee types.

Time statement

Last but not least, we are happy to introduce a new time sheet attestation feature, which lets employees confirm that their recorded hours are correct. The attestation messages can be customised by country and employee groups and hyperlink specific policies. This helps ensure compliance, especially in highly regulated industries. It also helps to ensure payroll accuracy, so employees are paid accurately and on time.

Timesheet attestation​

For a detailed review of everything included in the 2H 2024 Time Management release, be sure to check out Volker Ruof’s blog.

You can explore all the new enhancements for SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking and Employee Central Time Off in the 'What’s New Viewer.'

For additional release information and assets, visit the SAP SuccessFactors community page.

entry.title: Second Half 2024 Release: What’s New in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll?
entry.content:
Hi everyone! Can’t believe 2024 is almost over. Hopefully it is going as well for you as it is for our favorite product “SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll” 😉. It took center stage at the recently concluded SuccessConnect event in Lisbon, Portugal. Do watch the key sessions on-demand here including the keynote by SAP SuccessFactors’ President and Chief Product Officer Daniel Beck. I am excited to write this blog to cover the second half SAP SuccessFactors 2024 release! Have a look at the What's New Viewer for the full release notes and all of the details for everything we are delivering.

 

New Local Version – Croatia

With this release we are excited to now provide the ability to run SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll for Croatia. This now brings our localized versions to 52 native locales. (See the full list on our localization site with a cool interactive map). Native local country versions support our global base of customers with a local, compliant, and proven payroll platform. We also have five additional partner countries available as described in SAP KBA 3031216 - Employee Central Payroll: Availability of Partner Local Versions. EPI-USE has localized Peru, Vietnam, Denmark, Namibia, and Angola.

Our strategy is to deliver local versions we offered on-premise based upon customer and prospect demand so more to come for localizations. We also have a high interest from various partners to continue to localize more versions.


Croatia

For more information on this feature see the “What’s New Viewer”

 

Identity Provisioning Service for User Creation

Based upon customer request to simplify user creation, we have added the ability to use the Identity Provisioning service (IPS) in SAP Cloud Identity Services to create, update, and delimit users in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll instead of using the User Creation Report. Enabling this option creates a single System of Record for a user across all SAP systems simplifying the user lifecycle process. This particular request came from an influence request Customer Community Idea 277147.

For more information on this feature see the “What’s New Viewer”

 
Identity Provisioning

 

Enabling Certificate Revocation

It is now possible to block compromised certificates by known Certificates Authorities (CA’s) during authentication, thanks to this certificate revocation feature. This improvement simplifies certificate management and helps keep your environment secure by preventing unauthorized access

SAP's payroll operations will enable the certificate revocation in the Employee Central Payroll systems. For more information about the migration dates for your relevant data centers, check the Knowledge Base Article 0003519603

 

New Data Center – China

As of October 1, 2024, SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll is live in Shanghai Data Center. With this, we further complete our SAP SuccessFactors portfolio in the Greater China region and will accelerate your transition to the cloud.

For up-to-date information on SAP’s global network of data centers and the available cloud solutions portfolio there, please visit: https://www.sap.com/about/trust-center/data-center.html

 

What Else is New this Release?

There are a number of additional innovations available for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll in the second half 2024 release, including

  • Gender Replication for Mexico and United Kingdom
  • New Extensibility Option for Mapping of Onboarding Compliance Forms for Canada
  • Country and region-specific legal changes
  • And more

See the “What’s New Viewer” for more information on these features.

 

Where Can I Find More Information?

For more information, please watch the 2H2024 SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll release highlights session on-demand here (registration required)

Here are additional resources for those interested in learning more about SAP Payroll solutions

  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll Website
  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll SAP Help (Implementation) (Requires ID)
  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll Community Page (Discussion Forum) (Requires ID)
  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll Learning Journey (Educational Content) (Requires SAP Lea...
  • Other recent release highlights for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
    • 1H/2024 Release Highlight Blog
    • 2H/2023 Release Highlight Blog
    • 1H/2023 Release Highlight Blog 

For information on release highlights across SAP SuccessFactors HCM, visit this one stop shop for all product release and road map information.

entry.title: Second Half 2024 Release: What’s New in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Global Benefits?
entry.content:
The Second Half 2024 Release is here! I wanted to take just a couple of minutes to share with you some exciting news about SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Global Benefits.

As you may already know, the First Half 2024 Release introduced a new guided benefits enrollment experience to help simplify and streamline the experience for employees. Met with excitement by existing customers eager to adopt the new experience, and described as a game-changer by those evaluating SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Global Benefits, the refreshed enrollment experience makes benefits enrollment more seamless, simple and intuitive for employees.

However, we didn’t stop there! Building upon the user experience enhancements in our First Half 2024 Release, we continue to enhance the global benefits experience to make it easier for employees to enroll in and manage their benefits.

Refreshed Benefits Overview Page                                                                                                                 With the Second Half 2024 Release, we are excited to introduce a refreshed benefits overview page for insurance, savings, and pension benefit types. This new experience, available on desktop and mobile devices, provides employes with a single place to view their benefits details and take action such as declaring a life event, managing their beneficiaries, or enrolling in benefits. And, with convenient access from the ‘Manage My Benefits’ quick action card on the home page, the benefits overview enables employees to easily find a summary of their enrolled benefits, view their confirmation statement, and see available benefits.

Refreshed Benefits Overview Page (Desktop)

The updated Benefits Overview page consolidates benefits activities that were previously distributed across the employee profile and legacy benefits overview. As part of the SAP SuccessFactors Mobile application, the new benefits overview provides employees access to all the features of the desktop experience from the convenience of their mobile devices.

Refreshed Benefits Overview Page (Mobile)

Benefits Engagement Cards                                                                                                                                  In addition, we’ve further improved the benefits experience with new engagement cards to notify employees of time sensitive benefits-related tasks such as benefits enrollment windows for new hires, rehires and open enrollment. With a countdown of days remaining in the enrollment windows, these new engagement cards prompt employees to take action with simplified navigation to the enrollment process directly from the home page. 

Benefits Engagement Cards on the SAP SuccessFactors Homepage

But that’s not all… access the What’s new viewer to learn about all the innovations available in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Global Benefits with the Second Half 2024 Release.

For More Information                                                                                                                                          To learn about all the enhancements and innovations available across the SAP SuccessFactors HCM suite in the SAP SuccessFactors Second Half 2024 Release, check out the resources below:

  • Read the 2H 2024 Release Highlights Brochure

  • Visit the SAP SuccessFactors Community for release documentation, product videos, and solution area blogs

entry.title: Closing Zero-Balance Time Accounts to Hide from Employee Profiles Using SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Managing time accounts in SuccessFactors Time Management can sometimes require a customized approach, especially when the goal is to improve user experience by hiding time accounts with zero balances. This blog discusses a custom integration solution using SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) to close time accounts with zero balance, effectively hiding them from employees’ profiles when applying for absences.

Business Requirement

In our organization, there was a need to remove zero-balance time accounts from employee profiles to streamline the absence application process. While exploring options within SuccessFactors, we found no out-of-the-box solution to achieve this. As a result, we developed a custom integration to close these zero-balance time accounts using SAP Cloud Integration.

Key Considerations

Before implementing this solution, it’s essential to clarify the following requirements with the business stakeholders:

  1. Define Specific Time Accounts: Determine which time account types need to be closed at zero balance, so only these accounts are included in the interface configuration.

  2. Exclude Future Postings: The interface should close only those time accounts with a zero balance as of today’s date, ignoring any future-dated absences.

  3. Avoid Future-Dated Time Accounts: Exclude any time accounts with a future start date (e.g., those starting on January 1, 2025) from the interface, ensuring only currently active time accounts are included.

Solution Design: SAP Cloud Integration Flow

 

The following steps outline the integration flow (iFlow) developed to accomplish the closure of zero-balance time accounts in SuccessFactors.

Step 1: Set Properties

  • Content Modifier: Set the property “todaydate” to capture the current date.

 

Step 2: Retrieve Time Account Data

  • Request-Reply: Fetch time account data from SuccessFactors, filtering for active accounts where the booking start date is on or before today’s date.

 

Step 3: Filter Applicable Time Accounts

  • XSLT Script - Filter_Time_Accounts: This script is designed to filter only those time accounts that need to be closed when their balance is zero.

 

Step 4: Exclude Future-Dated Absences

  • XSLT Script - Exclude Future Dated Bookings: Excludes any time accounts with future-dated bookings, ensuring that only accounts with past or current dates are processed.

 

Step 5: Calculate Available Balance

  • XSLT Script - Calculate Available Balance: This script calculates the remaining balance for each time account. For example, if an employee has 16 hours and uses two 8-hour absences, the balance would reach zero (16 - 8 - 8 = 0).

 

Step 6: Filter Zero-Balance Accounts

  • XSLT Script - Filter Zero Balance: This script filters accounts with a zero balance, extracting relevant data fields like external code, user ID, and setting “accountclosed” to true.

 

Step 7: Split Records Per Time Account

  • Splitter: Splits the records by each time account to individually send closure details to SuccessFactors.

 

Step 8: Update SuccessFactors

  • Request-Reply: Posts the updated time account details back to SuccessFactors, closing each zero-balance account in the system.

 

Outcome in SuccessFactors

  • Before: Employees could see two time accounts with zero balances.

 

  • After: The integration closes only the active zero-balance time account (e.g., the current year’s account) while leaving future-dated accounts (e.g., Time Account 2025) unaffected.

 

 

This solution effectively hides zero-balance time accounts from employees, enhancing the user experience and ensuring they see only relevant information in their profiles. Through SAP Cloud Integration, organizations can better manage employee time accounts, keeping the interface clean and intuitive.

entry.title: SuccessFactors Time Off: Converting time into a Savings account (France)
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

Today @LSOKPA and I bring you a custom solution for a very common requirement in France – Converting time into a Savings account (untaken leave days can be saved for employee retirement).

Each year the company launches a campaign that requests employees to select the number of days they would like to move to a specific time account (CET) and/or to the retirement plan.

The request is sent to HR Administrators who view and validate the number of days selected.

The approved days should be deducted from the annual leave bucket and moved to the CET account.

Procedure:

To allow employees to make the request we created a custom MDF Portlet where the below info is displayed.

Employees are able to select the number of days they would like to move to the CET account and/or to the retirement plan:

The remaining balance is a view only field and it is populated by the following business rule:

The MDF custom portlet can have a workflow associated to require approval from the HR Administrator. For further information related to the custom portlet please refer to the SAP documention – Implementing the Metadata Framework.

In order to automate the deduction from the annual leave bucket and the posting on the CET account we built an Integration Center job:

The source data is our MDF custom portlet and the target would be twice the time account object as you can see highlighted above (red area is deducting the amount from the annual leave bucket | green area is posting the new amount to the savings account - CET).

And here is where the magic happens – in the custom portlet we have the time account external codes hidden – so they are not visible in the UI but stored in the database:

We map these 2 fields in the different time account external codes:

The time account details are filled in as follows:

 

 

And that brings us to the end of this article. I hope this information was useful for your projects.

Before adopting the solution please evaluate the pros and cons with your customer.

Looking forward to your comments and seeing your use cases/experiences on this topic.

entry.title: 10 Key Updates to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Best Practices in 2H 2024 Release
entry.content:
The second half of 2024 brings exciting updates to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Best Practices, which include enhancements across Employee Central Core, Global Benefits, Position Management, and Contingent Workforce Management. This latest release focuses on enhanced content, better user experience, new validations, and improved workflows. Let’s explore some key new features and how these changes will make the best practices content more relevant to our customers.

1. Reimagined Benefit Enrollment for Pensions

Continuing with the migration of USA Benefits from the legacy enrollment experience to the reimagined enrollment experience, Pension plans are included in the new experience in the 2H 2024 release. This means employees now have an intuitive, user-friendly interface when enrolling in pension plans, helping them make informed decisions about their retirement benefits. This change leaves us with the ‘Allowances’ and ‘Deductible Allowances’ benefits types that are still in the legacy experience—these will be included in the reimagined experience in the coming releases.

2. Employee Central Quick Actions for Employee and Manager Self-Services

Quick actions save time by allowing employees and managers to access frequently needed updates directly. This update introduces quick action templates, including:

  • Change Contract End Date
  • View Location
  • Change Chosen Name

These options allow employees and managers to make straightforward updates in an independent manner.

3. New Validation for New Hire's Age in Germany

A validation has been added for German hires to check whether a new hire is over fourteen years of age. This age validation ensures that organizations can meet their employment age requirements automatically out-of-the-box.

4. Field Criteria to Legal Entity in Position Object

In the Position object, the Cost Center field now includes the field criteria for Legal Entity, allowing cost center data to be filtered by the assigned legal entity. This feature enables more precise data maintenance and makes it easier for organizations to manage cost allocations across different legal entities. This update improves the integrity of HR and internal accounting practices for global companies with multiple legal entities.

5. Changes to Approvers in the Position Workflows

The Position Approval Workflows now incorporate the dynamic role “SFCC Org Design Administrator.” The base object for this dynamic role has changed from ‘Job Information’ to ‘Position,’ meaning organizations can assign dynamic groups to this role more flexibly. This change simplifies the workflows, allowing local approvers to be added as necessary and streamlining the position approval process. This change is an improvement over previous version and ensures that approval workflows align with the organizational design requirements.

6. Beneficiaries for the Latest Enrollment Experience

This enhancement allows the addition of beneficiaries in the new Reimagined Benefits enrollment process for the USA Benefits. With this update, employees can now configure beneficiaries for insurance, pension, and savings plans in real-time as they complete their benefits selections. This integrated approach simplifies the enrollment experience and improves transparency for employees.

7. New Opt-Out Feature

Offering employees the flexibility to opt out of specific benefits can be crucial for tailoring benefits to individual needs. The setting “Allow Waiving of Benefit” at the benefit level is maintained to ensure that employees can now opt out of specific benefits in the Reimagined Benefits Enrollment experience for the USA. This flexibility allows employees to avoid unnecessary benefits that may not suit their needs, offering a more personalized benefits experience.

8. Concurrent Employment Workflow Change

For organizations managing concurrent employees, simplifying workflows is essential to ensure efficient approvals and minimize delays. Previously, concurrent employment creation triggered an approval workflow for the target manager, which could have created bottlenecks. Now, based on the feedback we received, the system only sends a notification to the source manager, streamlining the process and improving efficiency in managing concurrent employment scenarios.

9. Validation for Duplicate Work Order IDs

Based on the feedback we got, there was a need to identify scenarios where an already-existing Work Order ID was used. With 2H 2024, a new validation triggers an error if an existing Work Order ID is entered for a contingent worker. This update helps prevent duplicate entries, ensuring data accuracy. A post-processing step in the Contingent Workforce Management Quick Guide now includes detailed instructions for business rules to assist implementation partners.

10. Sample Positions Have Country/Region Field Maintained

The Sample Employee Master Data provides demo organizational and employee data for running HR Business Processes in Employee Central. The sample data allows HR teams and implementation partners to test configurations, workflows, and integrations in a realistic environment, reducing the time needed to identify and resolve potential issues before going live. In the 2H 2024 release, we added the country/region data to all the sample positions. This change fixed an issue where some data could not be selected depending on the Country. e.g. Employee Class.

 

PS: This blog post does not cover the Time Management content in SAP Best Practices. You can review the What’s New Viewer for more details.

entry.title: Providing Compensation Information for a Higher Duty or Temporary Assignment
entry.content:
Providing Compensation Information for a Higher Duty or Temporary Assignment

In previous blogs, we outlined common scenarios in Higher Duty or Temporary Assignment, use cases for choosing the different types of Higher Duty or Temporary Assignment, and how to view manage workflows for the higher duty allowance.

A temporary assignment is when an employee works for a specific period temporarily within the boundaries of their existing contract for at least 1 day. When the temporary assignment position is of a higher classification or grade, it is termed as higher duty.

 

Introduction

When you create a higher duty or temporary assignment, you can provide the allowance computation basis in the Higher Duty or Temporary Assignment section. You do this by providing a value for the Allowance Computation Basis field.

In some cases, you may want to provide your own compensation information for the higher duty or temporary assignment. To provide your own compensation information for the higher duty or temporary assignment, choose the Others value for the Allowance Computation Basis field.

While this option allows you to provide your own compensation information for a higher duty or temporary assignment, it has a caveat. Because the standard solution does not provide replication of manually provided compensation information to the payroll system, you must implement a custom solution to handle this compensation information in your payroll system.

If you want the compensation information to be automatically calculated and replicated to the payroll system, then instead of the Others value, provide one of the following values for the Allowance Computation Basis field:

Base Salary Only: indicates that the reference salaries to compute the allowance must only include the basic salary.

Base Salary and Allowances: indicates that the reference salaries to compute allowance must include all pay components, including allowances.

If you want to directly provide the higher duty allowance information, then you can use the Nominal Reference Salary Adjusted Amount and the Higher Duty/Temporary Assignment Adjusted Amount fields in the employee's information to override these reference amounts that are automatically computed for the higher duty. The values provided in the Nominal Reference Salary Adjusted Amount and the Higher Duty/Temporary Assignment Adjusted Amount fields are replicated to the payroll system.

 

Summary

To summarize, we saw how you can provide compensation information for a higher duty or temporary assignment using the different options available for the Allowance Computation Basis field.  If you provide a custom compensation by choosing the Others option for the Allowance Computation Basis field, you must also implement a custom way to handle this information in your payroll system.

For more details about implementing higher duty or temporary assignment, check out the Implementing Higher Duty or Temporary Assignment guide.

Thank you for reading. I request you to share your feedback by leaving a comment.
